name: Pennsylvania
examName: Pennsylvania Real Estate Salesperson Licensing Examination

examFacts.notes: Two-part exam (80 national + 40 Pennsylvania state) administered by Pearson VUE for the Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission.

intro:
Pennsylvania scores its national and state-law portions separately, so a strong national score does nothing for you if the state half sinks you. That makes Pennsylvania-specific prep matter as much as the national material.

Pennsylvania tests its own rules in ways a national course never touches. Pennsylvania's Real Estate Licensing and Registration Act, the Consumer Notice and agency disclosure rules, the Real Estate Recovery Fund, the Real Estate Seller Disclosure Law, the Pennsylvania Human Relations Act, and the Commission's rules on advertising, escrow accounts, and prohibited conduct all show up on the state portion. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Pennsylvania-specific practice questions.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Pennsylvania real estate exam?
faq.a: The Pennsylvania salesperson exam has 120 scored questions split into two parts, an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Pennsylvania state portion. Pearson VUE administers both for the Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission, and the two sections are scored separately. A handful of unscored pretest questions are mixed in that don't count toward your result.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Pennsylvania real estate exam?
faq.a: You need 75% on each portion, scored separately. That works out to 60 of 80 correct on the national section and 30 of 40 correct on the Pennsylvania state section. If you pass one part and fail the other, you only retake the part you failed, so every question on your practice test matters.

faq.q: How much does the Pennsylvania real estate exam cost?
faq.a: The combined salesperson exam fee is $52 when you schedule the national and state portions together on the same order, which earns Pearson VUE's combination discount. Booked on separate orders they run $52 for the national part and $49 for the state part. Exam fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

faq.q: How long is the Pennsylvania real estate exam?
faq.a: Three and a half hours total. You get 150 minutes for the 80-question national portion and 60 minutes for the 40-question Pennsylvania state portion. The two sections can be scheduled back to back or on different days within your authorization period.

faq.q: Who administers the Pennsylvania real estate exam?
faq.a: The Pennsylvania Real Estate Salesperson Licensing Examination is administered by Pearson VUE for the Pennsylvania Real Estate Commission. You register and schedule your exam date through Pearson VUE.

faq.q: Is the Pennsylvania real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It has a reputation as one of the harder state exams, and the two-portion format is a big part of why. You have to clear 75% on both the national and the Pennsylvania law sections, and a strong national score won't rescue a weak state score. Most people who fail underestimated the Pennsylvania-specific material, the Real Estate Licensing and Registration Act, the Commission's rules, agency disclosure, the Recovery Fund, and the Seller Disclosure Law, and leaned too heavily on national principles. Honest practice on Pennsylvania-specific questions is the difference between passing on the first try and paying to retake a portion.

faq.q: What's on the Pennsylvania real estate exam?
faq.a: The 80-question national portion covers real property characteristics, ownership and transfer of title, valuation and appraisal, contracts and agency, real estate practice, property disclosures and environmental issues, financing and settlement, and real estate math. The 40-question Pennsylvania portion is weighted toward the rules that govern licensee activity (advertising, broker-salesperson relations, escrow accounts, and prohibited conduct), agency and disclosure, licensure, the Real Estate Commission and Recovery Fund, and miscellaneous topics like property disclosures, the Pennsylvania Human Relations Act, and condominium and planned-community law.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Pennsylvania real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish your 75-hour pre-license coursework, the highest-leverage thing you can do is grind through Pennsylvania-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. Generic real estate practice tests help with the national portion but won't catch you on Pennsylvania's licensing law, agency rules, and disclosure requirements. The RealReady app gives you the full Pennsylvania question bank, plus progress tracking and a missed-question mode so you can drill your weak spots. Most users study 30 to 60 minutes a day for a few weeks before sitting the exam.

The 20 Pennsylvania practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the Pennsylvania bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the Pennsylvania real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated Pennsylvania question bank built for the Pennsylvania real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the Pennsylvania law that trips people up, like the Real Estate Licensing and Registration Act, the Consumer Notice and agency disclosure rules, and the Real Estate Recovery Fund.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward Pennsylvania's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
